name: Holdings
summary: Retrieves the user's holdings (long-term DP/demat positions) for a given product. A POST call returning, on success, a JSON array of holding objects, each containing an exch_tsym array plus quantity and uploaded-price fields.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list.

json_fields.name: actid
json_fields.description: Account id of the logged in user.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: Holding request success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: exch_tsym
response_fields.description: Array of objects exch_tsym objects as defined below.

response_fields.name: upldprc
response_fields.description: Average price uploaded along with holdings

response_fields.name: exch_tsym.exch
response_fields.description: Exchange (field of the exch_tsym object in the values array).

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: Response received time (present in the failure response).

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: Error message (present in the failure response).

notes: On success the response is a JSON ARRAY of holding objects (not a single object); each object has its own stat and exch_tsym array. The failure response is a single JSON object with stat=Not_Ok plus request_time and emsg. The doc (page 42) gives two derived formulas: Valuation = btstqty + holdqty + brkcolqty + unplgdqty + benqty + Max(npoadqty, dpqty) - usedqty; Salable = btstqty + holdqty + unplgdqty + benqty + dpqty - usedqty. The exch_tsym sub-object fields (exch, tsym, token, pp, ti, ls) are documented on page 42 under "Exch_tsym object". The curl uses a placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/Holdings'; the real base URL is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI. The sample uses product prd="C" (CNC/cash). The failure-response field table on page 42 labels the stat description as "Position book request failure indication." — a doc copy-paste carryover from the position-book section, not Holdings-specific.
issues: The success-response sample only shows a subset of the documented fields (stat, exch_tsym, holdqty, colqty, btstqty, btstcolqty, usedqty, upldprc) — dpqty, npoadqty, benqty, unplgdqty, brkcolqty are documented but absent from the sample. The exch_tsym sample objects include exch/token/tsym but omit pp/ti/ls. None of the json_fields (uid/actid/prd) carry an explicit "Possible value" enum cell in the doc, though all three are marked required (trailing '*'); the sample uses prd="C". The failure-table stat description on page 42 says "Position book request failure indication" which is a doc copy-paste carryover, not Holdings-specific.
